name: Turmeric

book_claims.claim: Curcumin reduces chronic low-grade inflammation by inhibiting the NF-kB signaling pathway
book_claims.mechanism: Curcumin blocks IkB kinase, preventing NF-kB translocation to the nucleus and subsequent transcription of pro-inflammatory cytokines (TNF-alpha, IL-6, IL-1beta)
book_claims.study_ref: Aggarwal BB, Harikumar KB. Int J Biochem Cell Biol. 2009;41(1):40-59

book_claims.claim: Regular turmeric consumption is associated with lower rates of Alzheimer's disease in populations with high dietary intake
book_claims.mechanism: Curcumin crosses the blood-brain barrier and reduces amyloid-beta plaque accumulation
book_claims.study_ref: Mishra S, Palanivelu K. Ann Indian Acad Neurol. 2008;11(1):13-19

book_claims.claim: Curcumin bioavailability increases up to 2000% when consumed with piperine from black pepper
book_claims.mechanism: Piperine inhibits hepatic and intestinal glucuronidation of curcumin
book_claims.study_ref: Shoba G et al. Planta Med. 1998;64(4):353-356

supplementary_research.finding: Curcumin has strong evidence for reducing inflammatory markers (CRP, IL-6) and moderate evidence for joint pain relief comparable to NSAIDs in some trials
Meta-analysis of 8 RCTs: curcumin supplementation significantly reduced serum CRP levels (weighted mean difference: -2.20 mg/L, 95% CI: -3.96 to -0.44)
Systematic review found limited evidence for curcumin in Alzheimer's prevention in humans, despite promising animal studies. More large-scale RCTs needed.

culinary_pairings.source: Aloo gobi tradition, Yotam Ottolenghi, Plenty

seasonality: Year-round (dried); fresh root peaks autumn through spring

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Poorly absorbed alone (<1%). Requires piperine (20x boost) and/or fat for meaningful absorption.
Non-heme iron; pair with vitamin C for better absorption

synergies.description: Piperine increases curcumin bioavailability by 2000% by inhibiting glucuronidation
Fat-soluble curcuminoids require dietary fat; olive oil provides its own anti-inflammatory oleocanthal
Complementary anti-inflammatory mechanisms: ginger targets COX-2, turmeric targets NF-kB
